calendar.matters: Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Continued To Aug-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called for hearing together:
1) PTR-20-303765, Joanne Anderson Revocable Trust; and
2) PES-22-305372, Estate of Queenie Anderson.

Appearances:
Ray Robinson for trust beneficiary Josie Carter (via Zoom).
Phillip Campbell for administrator and successor trustee Ida Sadler (via Zoom).

At the outset, the Court inquired whether the Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account filed in the estate matter would resolve the trust issues. Mr. Campbell reported that the matters remained dependent on receipt of a property tax bill from the San Francisco Tax Collector and completion of federal and state tax returns, which would determine available funds for distribution. Mr. Campbell further stated that disputes existed between the parties regarding allocation of proceeds from jointly owned real property and identification of heirs, and requested a ninety-day continuance while awaiting the tax information.

The Court stated that the matters should proceed through case management.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matters to June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 and scheduled a Case Management Conference to occur on the same date. The Court directed the parties to submit a joint case management statement identifying the issues to be litigated, regardless of whether tax information had been received. The Court stated that a case management order would issue.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Jul-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for Josie Carter, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Phillip Campbell for Ida Saddler, Objector (via Zoom).

The Court noted that Mr. Robinson had not filed a written response to the objection filed by Ida Sadler, as previously discussed. Mr. Robinson stated it was his understanding he only needed to complete service on Ida Sadler in her capacity as administrator of a related estate, which he stated he had done.

The Court inquired why the related estate should bear the cost of a Heggstad petition when it appeared unrelated. Mr. Robinson argued that attorney's fees paid from escrow benefitted both the trust and the estate. Mr. Campbell disputed that assertion, stating the trust fees were separate and estate-related matters are still pending due to unresolved tax issues.

The Court and counsel discussed coordination with the related matter, Estate of Queenie Anderson, PES-22-305372. Mr. Campbell stated that a petition for final distribution had been filed in that matter, with a hearing date set for July 8, 2025. Mr. Robinson stated he had not received a copy of that petition. Mr. Campbell agreed to serve a copy of the petition. The Court decided to hear both the trust and estate matters together.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to July 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and rescheduled the hearing on the petition for final distribution in the related Estate of Queenie Anderson (PES-22-305372) for the same date and time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust And For Orders
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ray Robinson appeared via zoom for Joise Carter (petitioner).
Phillip Campell appeared via zoom for Ida Saddler (objector).

Discussion held regarding the objection that was filed, and that Mr. Robinson would like to respond to the objection. The matter is continued to June 16, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Assets In The Trust
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Joseph Fenton appeared by zoom for Ida Sandler (petitioner).
Ray Robinson appeared via zoom for Josie Carter.

Mr. Robison stated that his client waives notice issues. Petition for an Order conforming assets in the trust is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Assets In The Trust
Off Calendar Jul-03-2023 Continued To Sep-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order Finding And Correcting Scrivners Error In The Trust And For Confirmation Of Assets In The Trust
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Fenton, via ZOOM, for Ida Sandler, Successor Trustee.

The court and counsel discuss whether venue is proper. The court determines that venue is proper. The court expresses its concern regarding whether it can confirm the real property is an asset of the trust when the title is unclear. Mr. Fenton provides oral testimony to support his position regarding the percentage of ownership of the real property. The court advises Mr. Fenton that it is ready to take the matter under submission with the intent to grant only as to the scrivener's error and deny the Heggstad Petition without prejudice.

The Petition for an Order Finding and Correcting Scrivener's Error, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: J. Jeung. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Finding Of Nunc Pro Tunc As To The "Order On Petition Confirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets (Probate Code Section 850(A)(3)" Issued By This Court On April 6, 2021; For The Reissuance And Confirmation Of That Order As Appropriate; And, For Attorney?S Fees And Costs In An Amount To Be Determined By Proof
Off Calendar Jul-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Fenton appeared by zoom for Ida Sadler (Trustee).

The Court thanked Mr. Fenton for calling this issue of fraud to its attention. Mr. Fenton stated that he has had communication with the Carash Law Firm and with Attorney Domenico Scire, that his Client has contacted the State Bar, that the State Bar case number is 21- O 05617 and that the assigned Investigator is Suzanne Farrell. Mr. Fenton also stated that Mr. Scire assisted someone who aspired to be Ida Sandler the person named in the trust, that the signatures were all procured electronically and that his Client does not feel that Mr. Scire did anything wrong. In addition, Mr. Fenton stated that there is no Ida Sandler, that they believe it was a scrivener's error and that he understands that the order is now void ab initio. The Court noted that this order was issued based on misrepresentation to the Court and stated that it wants to get the order stricken as soon as possible. Mr. Fenton stated that he will be filing a petition to have his Client appointed as Successor Trustee and a heggstad petition hopefully by next week. Discussion was held regarding the risk involved with respect to the electronic process that is being practiced these days as well as procedures that need to be put in place to be able to properly identify parties and prevent fraudulent activity. The Court stated that it is prepared to issue an order that strikes the April 6, 2021 order, and that it reserves the issue of Attorney's fees. The Motion for a Finding of Nunc Pro Tunc as to the Order on Petition Confirming Successor Trustee and Trust Asset Issued by this Court on April 6, 2021, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.
